title: carex vulgaris subsp. alpina
intro: Carex vulgaris subsp. alpina, also known as alpine wood sedge, is a subspecies of the wood sedge plant, characterized by its narrow, grass-like leaves and brownish-purple spikes of flowers. Traditionally, Carex vulgaris subsp. alpina has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. Carex vulgaris subsp. alpina can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
